participants.name: 이상호
participants.professional_persona: 이상호 교수는 강원대학교에서 기계공학 연구단을 이끌고 있습니다. 지방 국립대학의 특성을 살려 지역 중소기업과의 공동 기술개발을 활성화하고 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 정밀가공 및 3D프린팅 분야에 전문성을 갖고 있으며, 연구 성과의 사업화 경험이 풍부합니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 연구단의 중간평가에 대비하여 성과지표를 체계적으로 관리하고, 지역 산업체 연계를 통해 대학원생의 취업률을 높이는 것이 목표입니다.

participants.name: 강원대학교에서
participants.professional_persona: 강원대학교에서 환경공학 박사 과정에 재학 중이며, BK21 혁신인재양성사업 교육연구단 소속입니다. 박사 과정생으로서 월 130만 원의 장학금을 받아 수질 오염 연구에 집중하고 있습니다. 지역 환경 문제 해결에 관심이 많으며, 연구 성과를 지자체 정책에 반영하는 것을 꿈꿉니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 수질 분석 및 환경 모니터링에 강점이 있으며, GIS를 이용한 오염원 분포 분석을 자유롭게 수행합니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 졸업 후 국립환경과학원이나 지방 환경기관에서 연구원으로 근무하며 지역 환경 개선에 기여하고 싶습니다.

participants.name: 의학과
participants.entity_name: Education Research Group Participants and Graduate Innovation Practitioners | KG-Gen-2kkble3r
participants.representative_type: Education Research Group Participants and Graduate Innovation Practitioners | KG-Gen-2kkble3r
participants.professional_persona: 의학과 박사 과정에 재학 중이며, 종양 면역학을 전공하고 있습니다. BK21 혁신인재양성사업의 지원을 받아 임상 샘플을 활용한 기초 연구를 진행 중입니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 유세포 분석 및 면역조직화학 염색 기술에 능숙하며, 동물 모델을 활용한 전임상 실험 설계에 강점이 있습니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 박사 학위 취득 후 의과대학 교수로 임용되어 기초 의학 연구와 함께 의사과학자로서의 역할을 수행하고 싶습니다.

participants.name: 사회학
participants.professional_persona: 사회학 박사 학위를 받고 영남권 대학의 BK21 교육연구단에서 연구 교수로 재직 중입니다. 디지털 전환과 노동 시장 변화를 연구하며, 대학원생들의 연구 방법론 교육을 담당하고 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 빅데이터를 활용한 사회 네트워크 분석 및 질적 연구 방법론에 정통합니다. 정부 출연 연구 과제 수행 경험이 다수 있으며, 정책 제안서 작성 능력이 우수합니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 데이터 기반 사회 정책 연구소를 설립하여 학문과 현장을 연결하는 연구를 주도하는 것이 목표입니다. BK21을 통해 후속 연구자 네트워크를 넓히고 싶습니다.

participants.name: 기계공학 (q5q1n8)
participants.professional_persona: 기계공학 박사 학위를 가지고 호남권 공과대학의 BK21 교육연구단에서 연구원으로 활동하고 있습니다. 로봇 매니퓰레이터의 정밀 제어 알고리즘을 개발하며, 지능형 로봇 경진대회 참가자들을 지도하고 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 임베디드 시스템 프로그래밍, 센서 융합, 실시간 제어 이론에 전문성을 보유합니다. 다수의 로봇 플랫폼 개발 경험을 통해 하드웨어-소프트웨어 통합 설계에 강점이 있습니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 자율 제조 시스템 분야의 창업을 준비 중이며, BK21 연구단의 산학 협력 네트워크를 활용해 시제품을 고도화하고 싶습니다. 장기적으로는 기술 기반 스타트업을 성장시키는 것이 목표입니다.

initial_posts.narrative: As the head of a mechanical engineering education research group at Kangwon National University, I estimate the total funding allocated to Hanyang University's BK21 program for 2022 based on the policy context. The total support budget for all 577 education research groups is 408,080 million KRW, but the education research group project budget is 355,151 million KRW. Hanyang University, being a large comprehensive university, likely receives a proportional share. Given that there are 200 future talent training groups and 205 innovative talent training groups, Hanyang likely hosts multiple groups. I estimate Hanyang's total funding at around 355,151 million KRW, which is the total for all groups, but this is a rough context number. For graduate scholarship slots, the policy states master's students receive 700,000 KRW/month, doctoral candidates 1,000,000 KRW/month, and doctoral students 1,300,000 KRW/month. With a typical group budget of around 1-2 billion KRW per year, and operating costs limited to 10%, the remaining 90% goes to personnel. Assuming an average stipend of 1,000,000 KRW/month per student, each slot costs 12 million KRW/year. With a group budget of 1.5 billion KRW, about 1.35 billion KRW for personnel, that supports roughly 112 students per group. Hanyang likely has multiple groups, but I estimate 200 slots for the entire university's BK21 program. For postdoc positions, the minimum salary is 3,000,000 KRW/month, so each postdoc costs 36 million KRW/year. With a portion of the personnel budget allocated to postdocs, I estimate 50 positions.
evidence: The policy context states the total education research group project budget is 355,151 million KRW for 577 groups, and Hanyang University, as a major institution, likely receives a significant share, but I use the total as a rough estimate for context.
Graduate stipend levels are fixed at 700K-1.3M KRW/month, and with operating costs capped at 10%, the remaining 90% of a group's budget funds personnel, allowing calculation of slot numbers.

The number of education research groups at Hanyang is not explicitly stated, but given the total of 577 groups across 68 universities, Hanyang likely hosts around 10-15 groups, each supporting 15-20 graduate students, leading to an estimate of 200 slots.
Postdoc positions are fewer due to higher salary costs (3M KRW/month minimum), and with limited budget, I estimate 50 positions based on typical group composition and funding constraints.

Evaluation panels are convened for annual, mid-term, and compliance purposes; given the policy specifies annual evaluations and mid-term evaluations in 2023, I expect 3 formal panel meetings in 2022 (annual, mid-term preparation, and compliance). Consulting sessions are offered to groups that request them; the policy states 280 out of 578 groups received consulting, so I estimate 280 sessions for Hanyang's proportional share. Compliance checks target the 205 innovative talent training groups, as per policy, so I estimate 205 checks. These estimates reflect my perspective as a group leader focused on meeting evaluation criteria and avoiding sanctions.

Reasoning centered on the policy's fixed two-installment payment schedule (70% in March, 30% in September) and explicit program specifications for evaluation, consulting, and compliance activities. No disagreements emerged, as all stakeholders—including education research groups, graduate students, early-career researchers, and a policy role—relied on the same policy documents and causal logic. The central tendency is a single point estimate for each variable, reflecting strong alignment on the deterministic nature of these activities within the BK21 program framework.

initial_posts.narrative: As a doctoral student in environmental engineering at Kangwon National University, I am part of a BK21 Innovative Talent Training group. From my experience, the outputs phase builds directly on the inputs and activities phases. With 200 graduate scholarship slots and 50 postdoc positions funded, and funds fully disbursed, I estimate that all 200 graduate students and 50 postdocs are actively supported in 2022. For publications, I know from my group that each graduate student typically produces 2-3 publications per year, and postdocs produce 3-4, but considering the early stage of the program and that not all students are equally productive, I conservatively estimate 2.5 publications per graduate student and 3 per postdoc, yielding about 600 total publications. For international conference presentations, about 20% of graduate students and 40% of postdocs present at international conferences annually, given funding constraints and the pandemic's lingering effects, leading to roughly 160 presentations. These estimates reflect my personal experience in a regional university group, where productivity is moderate but consistent.
evidence: From my own group, each graduate student publishes about 2-3 papers per year, and postdocs 3-4, but I use conservative averages of 2.5 and 3 to account for varying productivity across fields and universities.

International conference presentations are less common due to travel costs; in my group, about 20% of graduate students and 40% of postdocs present annually, which I apply to the 200 graduate and 50 postdoc slots.
The 200 graduate slots and 50 postdoc positions are fully utilized as per the inputs phase, and all funds are disbursed, ensuring active participation in research activities.
Compliance with reporting is high (575 out of 578 groups submitted reports), so I assume all supported participants are accounted for in output metrics.

initial_posts.narrative: As a medical doctoral student in oncology immunology at Hanyang University, I see the outputs phase as directly flowing from the inputs and activities phases. With 200 graduate scholarship slots and 50 postdoc positions funded, and all funds disbursed on schedule, I expect all 200 graduate students and 50 postdocs to be actively supported in 2022. For publications, based on my own group's experience and discussions with peers, each graduate student typically produces 2-3 publications per year, but many are still in early stages of their research, so I estimate an average of 2 publications per graduate student and 3 per postdoc, yielding about 500 total publications. For international conference presentations, I know that funding for travel is limited and many conferences are still hybrid due to the pandemic; I estimate about 20% of graduate students and 40% of postdocs present at international conferences, giving roughly 120 presentations. I note that 강원대학교에서 estimated 600 publications and 160 presentations, but I think that is optimistic for Hanyang's medical and science groups where many students are in clinical or lab-based research that takes longer to produce publishable results. My estimate is more conservative and reflects the reality of medical research timelines.
evidence: With 200 graduate scholarship slots and 50 postdoc positions funded, and all funds disbursed on schedule, I expect all 200 graduate students and 50 postdocs to be actively supported in 2022.

Based on my own group's experience, each graduate student produces about 2 publications per year on average, and each postdoc about 3, leading to roughly 500 total publications (200*2 + 50*3 = 550, but I round down to 500 to account for early-stage students).

International conference presentations are limited by travel funding and pandemic effects; I estimate 20% of graduate students (40) and 40% of postdocs (20) present, totaling 60, but I double that to 120 because some present multiple times per year.

My estimate is more conservative than 강원대학교에서's because medical and clinical research often has longer publication cycles, and I have firsthand experience with the time required to produce results from patient samples and animal models.

initial_posts.narrative: As an early-career researcher in sociology at a regional university in Yeongnam, I see the impact phase as the culmination of sustained investment in graduate talent development over a decade. The BK21 program's consistent funding of 200 graduate slots and 50 postdoc positions per year, combined with 280 consulting sessions and 205 compliance checks annually, has built a critical mass of highly trained researchers who are now advancing in their careers. These alumni, now in senior positions in academia, industry, and government, drive national innovation capacity by leading research teams, establishing collaborations, and mentoring the next generation. The dissemination of outstanding group achievements through the BK21 webzine and introduction booklets has spread best practices in research integrity, interdisciplinary collaboration, and operational know-how across institutions. This systemic change in research culture—emphasizing ethical conduct, open science, and cross-field partnerships—gradually lifts the entire S&T ecosystem. For South Korea's global S&T talent competitiveness rank, I estimate it will be around 8th in 2022, reflecting steady improvement from earlier years due to the BK21 program's long-term impact, though still behind top-tier nations like the US, China, and Germany. For high-impact publications per 1000 researchers, I estimate 12.5, based on the increased number of researchers trained through BK21 and the program's emphasis on quality over quantity, which leads to more top-cited papers. My perspective as a social scientist at a regional university makes me cautious about overestimating national-level impacts, as regional disparities in research capacity persist, but the program's focus on both metropolitan and regional universities helps narrow the gap.
